symptoms.symptom_name: Electric, shooting, dermatomal pain radiating from the lower back down one leg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Electric, burning, dermatomal leg pain is characteristic of nerve-root irritation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain provoked by weight-bearing with pain-limited inability to stand or walk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mechanical/positional provocation fits a radicular flare; severity does not by itself indicate a surgical emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known prior lumbar disc herniation / long-standing low back problem

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prior disc herniation raises the pre-test probability that this flare is recurrent radicular pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal bladder and bowel control; no saddle (perineal) anaesthesia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continence and intact saddle sensation on history make cauda equina very unlikely — the key patient-answerable exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new or progressive lower-limb weakness (pain-limited, not power-limited)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of progressive or bilateral motor deficit argues against significant central compression.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute complete disc extrusion with dense deficit
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No dense motor deficit or new reflex loss argues against a surgically urgent extrusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, night sweats, weight loss, or systemic illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: spinal epidural abscess or vertebral osteomyelitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of constitutional features lowers the probability of spinal infection or malignancy, though MRI remains the definitive exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Failure of maximum-dose OTC ibuprofen and paracetamol

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Analgesic failure indicates severity and drives management (neuropathic agent, review) but is not diagnostically specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Straight leg raise positive at 30 degrees on the affected side; hyperaesthesia over the L5 dermatome

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive SLR at a low angle with dermatomal hyperaesthesia is a strong sign of L5 nerve-root tension — sealed clinician exam, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Motor power 5/5 in the lower limbs, symmetrical reflexes, intact perianal sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal power, symmetrical reflexes and intact perianal sensation on examination exclude cauda equina clinically — sealed clinician exam; the AI Doctor relies on the history screen instead.

symptoms.symptom_name: MRI lumbar spine defining disc/root pathology

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: MRI is the definitive structural confirmation and excludes compressive emergencies — never obtainable via telehealth in real time.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: MRI lumbar spine (outpatient, if not improving or to characterise the disc/root)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the structural cause and excludes compressive pathology; appropriate as an outpatient GP-arranged investigation given the negative red-flag screen — not an emergency scan.
Reserved for a positive cauda-equina or progressive-deficit screen. Not indicated here because the red-flag screen is negative; ordering it now would reflect over-triage.
